title: decaneurum divergens
intro: Decaneurum divergens, also known as the yellow puccoon or wild crocus, is a perennial plant species native to North America, belonging to the Puccoon family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The health benefits of Decaneurum divergens include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a popular remedy for wound healing and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of the plant, typically made from its roots and rhizomes, can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and salves, and are often used to soothe and calm skin irritations.
